channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Texas Tech rallies from 16 points down to beat Arkansas 85-83 in first OT game in March Madness
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Darrion Williams could barely make a shot for the first 30 minutes. He didn't miss when it mattered most, sending Texas Tech to an improbable spot in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Will Richard and top-seeded Florida run away from Maryland's 'Crab Five' for 87-71 March Madness win
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Florida's three standout seniors and its deep bench kept bringing energy until it became too tough for Maryland's “Crab Five” to keep up. Coach Todd Golden could see his Gators wearing the Terrapins down.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flagg flies in March Madness and Duke wins 100-93 over Arizona to reach the Elite Eight
item.description: Duke stud Cooper Flagg put on a skills clinic and overcame an onslaught from Arizona and Caleb Love, finishing with 30 points, six rebounds and seven assists in a 100-93 NCAA Tournament victory that pulled the Blue Devils within one win of the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ovechkin leads entire Capitals team in tribute to Wild's Fleury with special postgame handshake line
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin didn't score for Washington, staying at 889 career goals and six away from breaking Wayne Gretzky's all-time record. Ovechkin did initiate a memorable moment on the ice in Minnesota on Thursday night, though.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Flyers fire coach John Tortorella in midst of another losing season
item.description: John Tortorella popped off after another Philadelphia loss — the sixth straight on his watch — and declared he was not interested in learning how to coach a losing team in another empty season. It's not his worry anymore.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jazz lose for 14th time in last 15 games, 121-110
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Alperen Sengun had 33 points and 10 rebounds, Amen Thompson finished with 16 points, 10 rebounds and five assists, and the Houston Rockets beat the Utah Jazz 121-110 on Thursday night. The Jazz have now lost 14 of its last 15 games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Juan Soto strikes out with 2 on to end Mets debut and 3-1 loss to Astros
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — With a chance to make a late splash in his New York Mets debut, Juan Soto came up empty. After signing the biggest contract in baseball history last offseason, the slugger came to bat with two runners aboard and the Mets down by two in the ninth inning Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL prospect Desmond Watson seeks to become heaviest player drafted in league history
item.description: Football linemen are typical among the biggest athletes on a team, but former Florida defender Desmond Watson's size and strength are exceptional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gosh! 'Napoleon Dynamite' star Jon Heder dishes out tater tots ahead of BYU's Sweet 16 game
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Napoleon Dynamite served tater tots on a plate instead of stuffing them in his pocket — and threw his Sweet 16 vote behind BYU.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: NBA and FIBA to take next steps toward adding a new European league, sides announce
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NBA may soon be significantly expanding its presence in European basketball, in the form of partnering with FIBA on a new league that the sides have been talking about for many years.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New San Diego Padres mural in North Park nears completion
item.description: A new San Diego Padres-themed mural in North Park replacing a previous work of public art that had been defaced earlier this year by fans of the rival Los Angeles Dodgers is nearing completion, just in time for opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: State audit reveals Anaheim doesn’t know condition of Angel Stadium on Opening Day
item.description: A report from the California State Auditor found that the city of Anaheim has not been able to ensure that Angel Stadium, one of the oldest stadiums in the majors, is being properly maintained.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: World Series champion has 1 major worry for the Mets going into the season
item.description: World Series champion AJ Pierzynski expressed concern over the New York Mets starting pitching going into the season during an appearance on OutKick's "Don't @ Me with Dan Dakich.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the San Diego Padres got their name
item.description: The San Diego Padres' name is a tribute to the city's history, with the team's name coming from the Franciscan Friars who founded the area.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Sam Merrill has found a home in Cleveland
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a homecoming for former Bountiful High and Utah State star Sam Merrill Sunday night. Merrill hit a couple of three-pointers in his return to his home state in Cleveland's 120-91 win over the Jazz. Merrill says loves playing in front of family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Late boxing legend George Foreman had deep ties to this East Bay city
item.description: (KRON) -- Boxing legend and former heavyweight champion George Foreman died last Friday at the age of 76. Foreman was born in Texas. However, after a troubled upbringing, he then moved to the West Coast after ninth grade when he enrolled in President Lyndon B. Johnson's Job Corps work program.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Power conferences box out 'Cinderella' for the first time
item.description: The Sweet 16, a popular destination for bracket-busting mid-majors, will be made up entirely of teams from power conferences, a first since the bracket expanded to 64 teams in 1985.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU fans celebrate Sweet 16 bid with Cougars
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Hours after BYU pulled out a 91-89 thriller over Wisconsin to advance to its first Sweet 16 since 2011, hundred of diehard BYU fans showed up at Provo Airport at 1:30 in the morning to welcome the conquering heroes back from Denver. "What a game!" said one BYU fan.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ryan Donato, Connor Bedard lead Blackhawks over Flyers 7-4
item.description: Ryan Donato scored twice, Connor Bedard had a goal and an assist, and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Philadelphia Flyers 7-4 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama rolls by Saint Mary's 80-66 to reach the NCAA Tournament's Sweet 16 for a 3rd straight year
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Alabama turned the page on the program's first Final Four appearance the moment the players received their commemorative rings in October. Coach Nate Oats knew his team needed to get a little deeper and more athletic, and find more balance to go even further this season.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Koby Brea leads the way as Kentucky beats Illinois 84-75 for first Sweet 16 appearance since 2019
item.description: Koby Brea matched a career high with 23 points and Kentucky beat Illinois 84-75 on Sunday to advance beyond the NCAA Tournament’s opening weekend for the first time since they reached the Elite Eight in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mitchell returns to Utah, leads Cavs past Jazz
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Jarrett Allen scored 18 points and Donovan Mitchell added 16, helping Cleveland snap a four-game losing streak with a 120-91 victory over the Utah Jazz on Sunday. Evan Mobley had 11 points, 11 rebounds and three blocks to help the Cavaliers get back in the win column.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Hall, Kitts rally top-seeded South Carolina to Sweet 16 in 64-53 March Madness win over Indiana
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Bree Hall had 11 points and Chloe Kitts scored all her 10 points in the second half as top seed South Carolina pulled away after trailing at halftime to beat ninth-seeded Indiana 64-53 on Sunday in the women’s NCAA Tournament and reach the Sweet 16 for the 11th straight time.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James' return from injury spoiled by Bulls' phenomenal effort in 146-115 win over Lakers
item.description: Coby White scored 36 points, Josh Giddey flirted with a quadruple-double and the Chicago Bulls wrecked LeBron James' return from injury with an impressive 146-115 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC wins scoring fest over Tampa Bay, 6-4
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Logan Cooley and Alex Kerfoot each scored twice and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning 6-4 at Delta Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia hires VCU coach Ryan Odom, whose father was a Cavaliers assistant
item.description: Williams named VCU coach Ryan Odom — whose father was a Cavaliers assistant and who handed the program its most historic and crushing defeat in the 2018 NCAA Tournament — as the full-time replacement to Tony Bennett on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE BARBER, Associated Press, Fox 5
